summary: This cluster investigates the nutraceutical potential of fermented brown algae, specifically *Laminaria japonica* (sea tangle), for addressing age-related metabolic and muscular decline in humans. The research focused on the effects of γ-aminobutyric acid (GABA)-enriched fermented sea tangle (GFST) on middle-aged women with sarcopenic obesity, a condition involving simultaneous muscle loss and fat gain. Key findings demonstrated that GFST supplementation significantly reduced total fat mass and triglyceride levels while increasing serum brain-derived neurotrophic factor (BDNF), angiotensin-converting enzyme, and human growth hormone. The study reported improvements in muscle strength and work performance, indicating that GABA-enriched algal fermentation products may exert an ergogenic and protective effect against sarcopenic obesity through BDNF-related pathways.
